title: Protocols.io Guidelines
author: Edwards V, David K
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This is a protocol for sharing protocols/workflows on protocols.io. We adapted many of recommendations used by Nature Protocols (available at:  https://www.nature.com/nprot/for-authors/preparing-your-submission ) to the existing fields in protocols.io. 

 There are some important qualities that we emphasized in these guidelines. For example, along with a summary description of the protocol, we recommended adding to the Abstract section an acknowledgement section as well as funding and conflicts of interest statement. In addition, to automatically generate keywords, we recommend using MeSH on Demand, where MeSH terms (“Medical Subject Headings,” a controlled vocabulary used to index life sciences research) are produced from copying/pasting the Abstract summary description. 

 This protocol is part of a manuscript, "An institutional approach to open science: operationalizing the sharing of biomedical research objects through relevant publication platforms," that is in preparation. This project was supported by funding from the Cancer Early Detection Advanced Research (CEDAR) center at Oregon Health & Science University’s Knight Cancer Institute.
